overview: The witest28-8 system is a single-component Hello World web application built as a Go HTTP service. It serves a static HTML page at the root path and handles all other routes with a 404 response. There are no inter-service dependencies; the entire application is self-contained within one deployable Docker-based service.
requirements: The root path (/) MUST return HTTP 200 OK with a valid HTML page displaying a 'Hello World' heading.
The HTML page MUST include <html>, <head>, <body> tags and a <title> element set to 'Hello World'.
The greeting message MUST be rendered as visible DOM text inside a heading element (e.g., <h1>).
The page MUST be responsive and legible on both desktop and mobile screen sizes.
The page MUST render correctly in Chrome, Firefox, Safari, and Edge.
Requests to undefined routes MUST return HTTP 404 Not Found with an appropriate response body.
The application MUST expose a /health endpoint returning HTTP 200 OK.
The application MUST NOT expose server version or internal path information in HTTP response headers.
The application MUST NOT accept or process any user-supplied input.
The page MUST fully load within 2 seconds on a standard broadband connection.
The page MUST meet WCAG 2.1 Level A accessibility standards including sufficient color contrast and meaningful document structure.
Content MUST be served over HTTPS in any non-local deployment environment (handled at ingress/platform level).

components.name: hello-world-service
components.openAPISpec:
openapi: 3.0.3 info: title: Hello World Service API description: Serves the Hello World web page and health endpoint. version: 1.0.0 paths: /: get: summary: Hello World page description: Returns the main Hello World HTML page. responses: '200': description: HTML page with Hello World greeting content: text/html: schema: type: string example: 'Hello World

Hello World

' /health: get: summary: Health check description: Returns 200 OK if the service is running. responses: '200': description: Service is healthy content: application/json: schema: type: object properties: status: type: string example: ok /{path}: get: summary: Catch-all 404 description: Returns 404 for any undefined route. parameters: - name: path in: path required: true schema: type: string responses: '404': description: Route not found content: text/html: schema: type: string
components.componentAgentInstructions:
## Component: hello-world-service ### Overview Implement a minimal Go HTTP server using the standard `net/http` package. The service listens on port **9090** and serves: 1. A Hello World HTML page at `GET /` 2. A health check JSON endpoint at `GET /health` 3. A 404 HTML response for all other routes --- ### Project Structure ``` /hello-world-service main.go Dockerfile go.mod ``` --- ### main.go Implementation Details **HTTP Server:** - Use `net/http` only — no third-party frameworks. - Listen on `:9090`. - Register handlers for `/`, `/health`, and a catch-all. **Route: `GET /`** - Respond with HTTP 200. - Set `Content-Type: text/html; charset=utf-8`. - Serve inline HTML (no template files needed). The HTML must: - Be valid HTML5 with `` - Include ``, ``, `` - Have `Hello World` in `` - Include a `` for responsiveness - Display the greeting in `

Hello, World!

` - Include minimal inline CSS for legibility and WCAG 2.1 Level A compliance: - Body background: `#ffffff`, text color: `#111111` (high contrast ratio > 4.5:1) - Font: system sans-serif stack - Centered layout with comfortable padding - Responsive: `max-width: 800px; margin: 0 auto; padding: 2rem` - NOT include any forms, inputs, or interactive elements **Route: `GET /health`** - Respond with HTTP 200. - Set `Content-Type: application/json`. - Body: `{"status":"ok"}` **Catch-all handler:** - For any route not matching `/` or `/health`, respond with HTTP 404. - Set `Content-Type: text/html; charset=utf-8`. - Serve a minimal HTML page with the message "404 – Page Not Found". **Security – Strip server headers:** - Wrap the default mux in a middleware that removes or replaces the `Server` header on every response (set to empty string or omit entirely) to avoid leaking Go/version info. - Do NOT include `X-Powered-By` or any similar headers. --- ### Dockerfile - Multi-stage build: - Stage 1: `golang:1.22-alpine` — build the binary with `CGO_ENABLED=0 GOOS=linux` - Stage 2: `gcr.io/distroless/static:nonroot` — copy binary only - Expose port `9090` - CMD: run the compiled binary --- ### go.mod - Module name: `github.com/witest28-8/hello-world-service` - Go version: `1.22` - No external dependencies --- ### Edge Cases - Route `/` must only match exactly the root — use a check like `if r.URL.Path != "/"` to fall through to a 404 when sub-paths are requested (since `net/http` ServeMux routes `/` as a catch-all prefix). - All methods (POST, PUT, etc.) to `/` should return `405 Method Not Allowed` — only GET is supported. - Health endpoint should also only accept GET. - The service must start and be ready to accept connections within 2 seconds of container start.
